questions.question: Explain the concept of function templates in C++ and how they achieve compile-time polymorphism.
keyPoints: Function template is a blueprint for creating functions with generic type parameters
Uses 'template<typename T>' or 'template<class T>' syntax
Compiler generates type-specific code during instantiation
Achieves polymorphism at compile-time without runtime overhead
Different from runtime polymorphism using virtual functions

questions.question: What is template argument deduction? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Template argument deduction is automatic type determination by compiler
Compiler examines function arguments to infer template parameters
Example: max(10, 20) deduces T=int automatically
Can be overridden with explicit template arguments: max<double>(x, y)
Makes generic functions convenient to use without type specification

questions.question: Differentiate between function template overloading and function template specialization.
keyPoints: Overloading: multiple functions with different signatures coexist
Specialization: provides specific implementation for particular types
Overloading doesn't require template keyword
Specialization uses template<> syntax for specific type
Overloading resolved at compile-time based on argument types

questions.question: Write a function template to find the maximum of two values and demonstrate its usage with integers and doubles.
keyPoints: Template definition: template<typename T> T maximum(T a, T b)
Return (a > b) ? a : b
Called with ints: maximum(5, 10) returns 10
Called with doubles: maximum(3.14, 2.71) returns 3.14
Compiler generates separate instantiations for each type

questions.question: What is SFINAE? How is it useful in generic programming?
keyPoints: SFINAE stands for Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
It is a template metaprogramming technique
Enables compile-time selection of function overloads
Works by making certain functions invalid for specific types
Used via enable_if, decltype, or void_t techniques
Allows type-trait based function enabling/disabling

questions.question: Explain non-type template parameters with an example.
keyPoints: Non-type template parameters accept constant values, not types
Common types: int, pointers, references, std::size_t
Example: template<int N> struct Array { int arr[N]; }
Used for compile-time constants and array sizes
Value must be known at compile-time
Enables static polymorphism and optimization

questions.question: What happens when both a regular function and a function template match equally well? Which one is preferred?
keyPoints: Non-template function is preferred over template when both match equally
This is specified by C++ standard (overload resolution rules)
Template is considered only if no exact match exists
Explicit specialization takes precedence over implicit instantiation
Example: swap(int&, int&) regular vs template version - regular wins

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program demonstrating function templates with: (a) basic swap function (b) array sorting function (c) special implementation for character arrays. Include necessary explanations.
keyPoints: Basic swap template: template<typename T> void swap(T& a, T& b) { T temp = a; a = b; b = temp; }
Array sort template: template<typename T> void sortArray(T arr[], int n) using bubble sort or similar
Specialization for char: template<> void swap<char*>(char*& a, char*& b) for string literals
Demonstrate usage with int, float, and char* arrays
Show that specialization overrides generic template for char*
Include proper headers and main() function

questions.question: Explain function template specialization and partial specialization in C++. Write a program to demonstrate full specialization for finding the minimum of two values where string comparison differs from numeric comparison.
keyPoints: Full specialization: template<> return_type func<specific_type>(params) { }
Full specialization replaces entire template for one type
For strings, need special compare logic (lexicographical)
Numeric types use < operator directly
Demonstrate with int and std::string
Show how specialization provides custom behavior for specific types
Include proper includes and explain overload resolution

questions.question: Design and implement a generic function that can add two containers (like arrays or vectors) element-wise. The function should work with any numeric type. Also explain how you would extend it to handle mixed types (e.g., adding int and double).
keyPoints: Generic addContainers template taking two containers
Template<typename T1, typename T2> for mixed types
Return type can be decltype(a[0] + b[0]) or explicit
Handle different container types using iterators or size
For mixed types: template<typename T1, typename T2> auto add(T1 a, T2 b) -> decltype(a+b)
Demonstrate with vector<int>, vector<double>, mixed int+double
Consider return type promotion rules
Include appropriate error handling for size mismatch

questions.question: What is the difference between class templates and function templates? Explain with examples. Also discuss when to use which.
keyPoints: Function templates support automatic type deduction, class templates usually require explicit type
Class template: template<typename T> class Stack { }; Requires: Stack<int> s;
Function template: template<typename T> T max(T a, T b); Called as: max(5, 10)
Class templates can have default template arguments for types
Function templates can be overloaded, class templates use specialization
Use function templates for algorithmic operations on values
Use class templates for data structures and container types
Class templates support member function templates
